name: Kiwi Health Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the yeast, sugar and 1/2 cup warm water together in a bowl and set aside for about 10 minutes until the mixture is quite frothy.
2. In a large bowl, mix together the flour, wholemeal flour, salt, sesame seeds, sunflower seeds and caraway seeds and make a well in the center.
3. Stir the honey, molasses and 900 ml very warm water into the frothy yeast mixture.
4. Pour into the well and mix with a wooden spoon until almost combined.
5. Turn out onto a floured surface and knead for 5 minutes. Make sure you have extra flour on hand to stop the dough sticking.
6. Turn the dough into a greased bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Cover the bowl with a towel and let stand in a warm place for 20 minutes, or until doubled in size.
7. Turn the dough out and knead for another 5 minutes until it is smooth and elastic.
8. Divide into 3 equal portions and shape into loaf shapes. Place into three greased 20cm x 10cm loaf tins.
9. Cover and leave until doubled in bulk. This should take about 20 minutes.
10. Bake at 200°C for 35-40 minutes or until the loaf sounds hollow when tapped underneath. Turn onto a cake rack to cool.
